LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TRADER JOE'S COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT - CCP 430.41 REQUIRES A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER MAY BE FILED. COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES THAT SUCH A MEET-AND-CONFER HAS NOT OCCURRED HERE, SO THIS DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR TO ALLOW COMPLIANCE. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT HAS UNTIL AUGUST 19, 2019 TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED.

Off Calendar Sep-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CARL E. KADLIC, Esq. of Law Offices of Carl E. Kadlic (Tel: 925-726-3197) for plaintiffs;
Atty. JOHN K. SALCEDO, Esq. of Tyson & Mendes LLP (Tel: 628-253-5070) for defendant Trader Joe's Company.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS GAYLE HALL and JAMES HALL's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Trader Joes Company.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Motion is granted in full. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,500.00 against defendant Trader Joe's Company (not against defense counsel) for failure to make discovery.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at greg@greggroeneveldlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Greg Groeneveld; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Trader Joes Company
Off Calendar Sep-01-2021 Continued To Sep-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Continued To Oct-05-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-25-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Carl E. Kadlic, Esq. of Law Office of Carl Kadlic, 925.726.3197, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Yuri Yang, Esq. of Schumann Rosenberg & Arevalo LLP, 714.850.0210, appearing for the Defendant 1401 California Street LLC.

DEFENDANT 1401 CALIFORNIA STREET LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter called, ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant 1401 California Street LLC's motion to strike the prayer for "prejudgment interest according to law" in the complaint is denied. Defendant does not establish as a matter of law that Plaintiff cannot recover "damages certain or capable of being made certain by calculation" on any of her claims. (Civ. Code sec. 3287(a); see, e.g., Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1004, 1009-1010 ["Prejudgment interest may be awarded under section 3287 'even if it is not specifically authorized by the statute underlying the plaintiff's claims'"].)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Continued To Nov-20-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TRADER JOE'S COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

Defendant Trader Joe's Company's demurrers are overruled. The complaint adequately pleads claims under the Unruh Civil Rights and California Disabled Persons acts. A complaint need only provide "notice of the issues sufficient to enable preparation of a defense." (Doe v. City of Los Angeles (2007) 42 Cal.4th 531, 549-550.) Unruh and Disabled Persons claims are not among the few that require particularized pleading. (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 6-46 et seq.) The few loose references to pleading statutory claims with specificity trace back to suits against public entities, which Trader Joe's is not. (See Lopez v. Southern Cal. Rapid Transit Dist. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 795.) Whether the fourth cause of action is labelled "negligence" or "negligent infliction of emotional distress" is of no moment.

Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Continued To Oct-16-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-05-2019 Continued To Sep-04-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TRADER JOE'S COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

CCP 430.41 requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer may be filed. Counsel's declaration indicates that such a meet-and-confer has not occurred here, so this demurrer is ordered OFF CALENDAR to allow compliance. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant has until August 19, 2019 to respond to the complaint.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
